title: Tool
description: A tool registry entry. Tools are the leaf actions a role may invoke. Each entry names the risk level, who may call it, whether approval is required, which paths are off-limits, which events fire on call, and the shape of the result.

id.description: Namespaced tool identifier. Format: <category>.<action-slug>. Example: repo.apply_patch, tests.run, deploy.production.

description.description: One-line description of what the tool does.

risk_level.description: Blast radius if the tool misfires. Critical tools should require approval and narrow allowed_roles.

allowed_roles.description: Role ids permitted to call this tool. The policy engine cross-checks against the role's allowed_tools list.

requires_approval.description: If true, the harness must collect a human (or higher-privileged role) approval before invoking the tool.

forbidden_paths.description: Glob patterns the tool must never read, write, or otherwise touch. Used by the harness as a final guard.

emits_events.description: Event type names (matching the type field in event.schema.json) that fire when the tool runs.

output_schema.description: Reference to the schema describing the tool's result. Either an artifact schema URL (https://athena.dev/ops/schemas/artifact.schema.json) or a sibling result schema URL.

category.description: Coarse category for routing and dashboards.

rate_limit.description: Throughput cap enforced by the harness.

calls_per_minute.description: Maximum invocations per minute across all callers.

calls_per_hour.description: Maximum invocations per hour across all callers.

network_required.description: Whether the tool requires outbound network access. Sandboxes without network must reject the tool.
